blockers.issue: The TechSpec carries roughly 20 lines of Go signatures across the entire document (CursorStore plus three minimal structs). Missing types include CursorKey, Cursor, AdvanceCursor, CursorQuery, Reference, RunSummary, TimelineItem, TaskRuntimeLimits, the new task.Service methods for summary recording, max-runtime watchdog transitions, spawn-failure breaker increment/reset/circuit-open, current_run_id projection writers, scheduler health telemetry, the coordinator's deterministic skill-injection interface, and the situation context-bundle assembler. This is exactly the prose-only shape that L-012 calls pre-rejected.
blockers.rationale: L-012 (TechSpec prose-only rework) requires concrete Go interface signatures pasted as code blocks; spec readiness markers 3 + 5 are not satisfied. Implementation will diverge per agent and force multiple review rounds, which contradicts the multi-LLM-pipeline and two-touch-rule postures.
blockers.suggested_fix: Paste signatures for: every new task.Service method (RecordRunSummary, EnforceMaxRuntime, IncrementSpawnFailure, ResetSpawnFailure, OpenSpawnCircuit, MaintainCurrentRunID per transition), CursorStore companion types, scheduler health/recovery contract, coordinator skill-injection contract, situation TaskContextBundle assembler, and the request/response payload structs for the new contract fields. Match the autonomy techspec exemplar cited in L-012.

blockers.section: Implementation Design / Core Interfaces (CursorStore) + Architectural Boundaries invariant 11
blockers.issue: The CursorStore interface defines Advance(ctx, update) but the TechSpec does not specify how delivery confirmation and cursor advancement are made transactionally consistent. There is no statement on whether the consumer wraps delivery+advance in its own SQLite tx, whether Advance accepts a delivery-confirmation callback, whether Advance enforces server-side monotonicity (rejecting lower sequences), or how partial-delivery semantics are handled.
blockers.rationale: ADR-003 explicitly lists this as a risk: 'If cursor advancement is not transactional with delivery confirmation, consumers may drop or duplicate notifications.' Leaving the contract implicit re-creates the bug class the primitive was introduced to prevent. Lease/safety invariants must be enumerated (L-012 rule 6, L-008 reopen-after-restart discipline).
blockers.suggested_fix: Add a numbered invariant section for the cursor primitive: monotonic-advancement enforced server-side, Advance refuses non-monotonic updates with a typed error, callers commit delivery and Advance inside the same store transaction (or supply a confirmed-delivery callback), reset is the only path that lowers a cursor. Paste the exact Advance/Reset signatures so this is unambiguous.

blockers.section: Architectural Boundaries / Lease, ownership, and delivery safety invariants (item 6) + Data Models / tasks.current_run_id
blockers.issue: ADR-005 mandates that current_run_id be set/cleared inside the same transaction as the corresponding run transition and lists nine touch points (claim, start, complete, fail, release, cancel, recovery, synthetic-terminal creation, task closure). The TechSpec body never enumerates these update sites as a numbered invariant list. There is also no statement of which method on task.Service is the single owner of the projection write.
blockers.rationale: L-005 and L-003 require the projection to never become a second authority; L-012 rule 6 requires safety invariants as a numbered list. Future implementation tasks will drift transitions silently because the invariant is not in the body of the spec. The 'current_run_id becomes accidental authority' Known Risk is real and not mitigated by repeating prose.
blockers.suggested_fix: Add a numbered invariant block: 1) ClaimNextRun → set; 2) CompleteRunLease → clear; 3) FailRunLease → clear; 4) ReleaseRunLease/ReleaseSessionRunLeases → clear; 5) RecoverExpiredRunLeases → clear; 6) Synthetic terminal run → set then clear; 7) Task close/archive → clear; 8) Cancel → clear. State that task.Service is the sole writer and that the column is updated inside the same store tx as the run transition.

blockers.section: Implementation Design + Test Strategy (Unit Tests, internal/task)
blockers.issue: The TechSpec mentions 'synthetic terminal paths' only in test strategy and ADR-005, but never declares whether the Hermes-style synthetic zero-duration run on never-claimed force-complete is in MVP scope, what triggers synthesis, which fields it populates, or where the path lives. The Executive Summary's 'Core 1-7 from the synthesis' would include item #3 (synthetic terminal run), so this is currently a hidden in/out boundary.
blockers.rationale: Greenfield-delete (L-006) requires explicit scope. A test that depends on a behavior the spec does not define produces partial-surface completion (no contract DTO, no API copy, no docs), which is forbidden by the 'No partial-surface completions' rule in internal/CLAUDE.md.
blockers.suggested_fix: Either declare the synthetic-terminal-run path in scope and specify the trigger (CompleteTask/FailTask on a task with no active run), the field shape (started_at == ended_at, claimed_by_kind=origin actor, summary preserved, attempt counter rule), the owning method on task.Service, and the contract surface; OR mark it explicitly out of scope and remove the test-strategy reference.

blockers.issue: The spec adds spawn_failure_count and last_spawn_error columns but does not specify the policy: which FailRunLease outcomes increment the counter, the reset condition (success on next claim? operator clear?), the breaker-open transition target (does the task move to a new status? does ClaimNextRun filter? does it surface as a typed event?), or the actor split between task.Service (which 'owns' the counter per the boundary table) and scheduler (which observes spawn outcomes).
blockers.rationale: L-012 rule 4 requires data-model field rationale (purpose + shape) per new column, and rule 6 requires safety invariants as a numbered list. Without these, the breaker becomes a generic retry knob — the exact risk ADR-002 calls out.
blockers.suggested_fix: Specify: (a) the typed error classes that increment (spawn_failed, session_unreachable, provider_auth); (b) reset on first successful claim, owned by task.Service; (c) breaker-open behavior — name the new task status or filter applied to ClaimNextRun, plus the typed event emitted; (d) where the increment is called from (FailRunLease wrapper, never from scheduler); (e) the canonical telemetry event already listed (task.spawn_failure_circuit_opened) tied to the transition.

blockers.section: Architectural Boundaries (5 + 7) and Implementation Steps step 9 (Scheduler health and max-runtime recovery)
blockers.issue: Max-runtime enforcement requires four authoritative actions: detect expiry (scheduler observation), request managed stop on the session (session manager), terminate the subprocess, write terminal state (task.Service.FailRunLease). The TechSpec says 'scheduler triggers max-runtime recovery' and 'task service owns max-runtime enforcement transitions' without sequencing the actor split. If scheduler ends up calling FailRunLease/ForceTaskStop directly or the recovery sweep writes terminal state outside task.Service, this collapses authority back to the scheduler and violates the Authoritative-Primitive Exclusivity rule.
blockers.rationale: L-005 (Authoritative primitives are exclusive) and L-003 (task_runs single queue) — scheduler may observe and wake/sweep, but it cannot mutate terminal state. The current wording leaves the load-bearing sequence unstated.
blockers.suggested_fix: Add a sequence diagram or numbered list: 1) scheduler observation detects started_at + max_runtime_seconds < now; 2) scheduler emits a max-runtime-exceeded recovery request via a typed interface owned by task.Service; 3) task.Service requests managed stop on the session; 4) session manager invokes process-group SIGTERM → grace → SIGKILL via internal/procutil; 5) task.Service writes FailRunLease(reason='timed_out'). State explicitly that scheduler never calls FailRunLease/ForceTaskStop and never writes claim/lease state.

blockers.section: Architectural Boundaries (Coordinator) + ADR-006 deterministic injection mandate
blockers.issue: ADR-006 mandates that agh-orchestrator be loaded deterministically by the coordinator runtime during bootstrap, separate from coordinator.PromptOverlay's runtime facts. The TechSpec says skills 'live in internal/skills/bundled' and the coordinator 'loads deterministic agh-orchestrator skill content' but does not specify the loader interface, the path through internal/coordinator + internal/daemon/coordinator_runtime that injects the content, where the skill body is read (registry vs. direct embed), or how the prompt-vs-skill split is enforced (no duplicate guidance).
blockers.rationale: L-012 rule 3 (concrete signatures) — coordinator integration is hidden coupling without an interface. ADR-006's 'duplicate or conflicting guidance between PromptOverlay and the skill body' risk is mitigated only by 'tests' rather than by a contract.
blockers.suggested_fix: Paste the loader interface (e.g., BundledSkillLoader or the existing skills.Registry.Load entry point), name the call site in coordinator_runtime that consumes it, and define the assembly contract (skill body precedes overlay; overlay carries only runtime-bootstrap facts). Add a numbered invariant: skill body is the only source of stable guidance; overlay must not duplicate it.

blockers.section: API Endpoints / GET /api/agent/context + Implementation Design / TaskContextBundle
blockers.issue: The TechSpec extends /agent/context with a TaskContextBundle but does not specify the binding rule: which task the bundle resolves to (caller session's currently-claimed run? operator-supplied task_id query param? the session's last claim if there is no active claim?), what happens when the caller has no active claim, or how Reference is populated from session identity.
blockers.rationale: Partial-surface completion is forbidden by internal/CLAUDE.md. Without binding rules, transports cannot validate inputs, redaction cannot be tested, and the agent-manageable surface is incomplete. This is also a security-relevant choice — wrong binding lets a session see another session's claim context.
blockers.suggested_fix: Specify: bundle resolves through session-bound active-lease lookup (LookupActiveRunForSession); if no active lease, the bundle is empty (or returns the most recently-claimed terminal run, explicitly chosen); operators using the operator UDS path may pass an explicit task_id; redaction tests cover the no-claim case and the cross-session attempt.

blockers.issue: The spec promises that 'task read payloads expose a seed cursor so web clients can start SSE without a race' but never names the contract field, its type, or its relationship to task_events.event_seq. The same omission applies to list payloads, where the synthesis explicitly recommends standardizing latest_event_seq across event-bearing GETs.
blockers.rationale: L-012 rule 4 (field rationale per new contract field) and the agh-contract-codegen-coship rule — generated OpenAPI/TS types cannot be regenerated without a named field. Web SSE race elimination is one of the spec's stated goals; leaving the field anonymous makes implementation tasks ambiguous.
blockers.suggested_fix: Name the field (e.g., latest_event_seq), specify type (uint64), state that every event-bearing list/read payload exposes it sourced from task_events.event_seq, and add a contract test that web clients pass it as ?after_sequence on EventSource open. Co-ship openapi/agh.json + web/src/generated/agh-openapi.d.ts in the same change.

nits.issue: All ADRs are dated 2026-05-05 but the task is being authored on 2026-05-04.
nits.suggested_fix: Reconcile dates so ADRs do not appear to predate the TechSpec they are referenced from.

nits.issue: 'Prove scheduler never claims work' is goal-shaped, not test-shaped.
nits.suggested_fix: Specify a boundary test (interface-shape assertion or import-graph check) that fails if the scheduler package imports claim methods or writes to task_runs ownership columns.

nits.issue: Listed canonical events do not state their exposure path (typed observability contract, /api/observe stream, structured logs).
nits.suggested_fix: For each event, name the contract event kind in internal/observe and confirm coverage-matrix test entries are added in the same change.

nits.issue: Bundled skill frontmatter (RFC-002 metadata.agh.* fields, always_load triggers, skill version) is not specified.
nits.suggested_fix: Paste the frontmatter shape for agh-task-worker and agh-orchestrator, including always_load conditions, version, and any related_skills declarations.

nits.issue: DB column is INTEGER seconds; config key is a duration string. Two surfaces, two shapes.
nits.suggested_fix: Document the conversion site (config layer parses duration → seconds before reaching task.Service) and assert validation rejects negative values, fractional seconds, or unbounded durations.

nits.issue: Field list is given but PK shape, indexes, and unique constraints are deferred to implementation.
nits.suggested_fix: Specify PK = (consumer_id, stream_name, subject_id), partial index on (stream_name, last_sequence DESC), and the rationale for that key shape (independent consumers must not block each other, per ADR-003).

nits.issue: Generated artifact names are not enumerated, despite the agh-contract-codegen-coship rule.
nits.suggested_fix: List openapi/agh.json + web/src/generated/agh-openapi.d.ts as co-ship targets in the same PR; reference the make codegen-check gate.

nits.issue: Spec does not state whether agh-task-worker is always_load on every spawned worker session vs. only on session types with an active task claim.
nits.suggested_fix: Choose the trigger explicitly and state the rationale (RFC-002 lifecycle hook or always_load on the worker session type).

nits.issue: New transitions (summary recorded, breaker opened, max-runtime exceeded, current_run projection updated, cursor advanced) are not mapped to call-site hook dispatch.
nits.suggested_fix: For each new transition, name the typed hook taxonomy entry and the call site that dispatches it; restate the rule that hooks never tail event tables.

nits.issue: Web list of impacted files is partial and Docs impact bullets do not name specific MDX pages.
nits.suggested_fix: Enumerate web/src/systems/tasks files exhaustively (live drawer, dashboard health card, run drawer summaries, generated payload mapper) and name the packages/site/docs/runtime/*.mdx pages so cy-web-docs-impact items are testable.

summary: ADRs 001-006 lock the right invariants (single queue, exclusive ClaimNextRun, projection-only current_run_id, instructional skills, durable cursor), but the TechSpec body fails the L-012 quality bar — concrete Go signatures, numbered safety invariants, and field rationale are mostly absent, and load-bearing sequencing for synthetic-terminal runs, spawn-failure breaker policy, max-runtime enforcement actor split, /agent/context binding, deterministic skill injection, cursor advancement transactionality, and the SSE seed cursor field name is unspecified. The spec must add explicit signatures, invariant lists, and actor sequences before tasks generate cleanly; otherwise implementation will drift across reviews and risk re-introducing parallel-authority bugs the ADRs explicitly forbid.
